hypotheses.label: Enhanced CSS Overlay
hypotheses.statement: This stack is optimal for this D/L/A structure because SpeechBubbleContent (obj-cd-01) and WorldToScreenProjection (obj-cd-09) are already the canonical DATA and LOGIC entities for content delivery. Enriching speech-bubble.ts with CSS-native animations, phase-specific signalling icons, and CognitiveLoadGuard (obj-cd-10) achieves the Mayer CTML improvements — spatial contiguity via character-following, segmenting via existing beats — at <10% migration cost and zero impact on the WebGL render loop.

dlaJustification.DATA: SpeechBubbleContent (obj-cd-01) is the authoritative teaching content struct. Extension with optional animationHints is non-breaking. No new DATA entities required.
dlaJustification.LOGIC: WorldToScreenProjection (obj-cd-09) retained as-is. CognitiveLoadGuard (obj-cd-10) and MayerPrincipleChecker (obj-cd-11) added as derived-property logic consuming existing TeachingBeat (obj-cd-02). ForwardProp chain: TeachingBeat → SpeechBubbleContent → speech-bubble.ts → CSS DOM.
dlaJustification.ACTION: EnterAdvancesBeat (obj-cd-14) and SpeechBubbleFollow (obj-cd-15) unchanged. SpatialSignalling (obj-cd-18) added as additive CSS animation action — no WebGL mutations.
dlaJustification.LEARN: ContentEngagement (obj-cd-19) instrumented via DOM events. FSRSScheduling (obj-cd-20) unchanged.

testCriteria: CSS-native animation adds <1ms per-frame overhead in Chrome DevTools performance profiler
SpeechBubbleContent optional animationHints extension does not break teaching-flow.ts beat sequencing
60fps maintained with full n8ao + postprocessing pipeline and enhanced speech bubble in prod build
CognitiveLoadGuard correctly suppresses extraneous vocabulary per Coherence principle (clm-ext-23)
architectureImplications: speech-bubble.ts enhanced with CSS keyframe animations per TeachingBeat.phase
SpeechBubbleContent gains optional animationHints, modalityFlags fields
CognitiveLoadGuard (obj-cd-10) implemented as derived property — no pipeline change
No Three.js scene graph changes, no new npm dependencies

devcon5Alignment.ddd: SpeechBubbleContent domain-driven — CSS overlay is rendering surface, not domain concept
devcon5Alignment.dry: Single speech-bubble.ts — no parallel rendering paths created
devcon5Alignment.ocp: Open for animation/modality extension via optional fields; closed for core CSS rendering contract
devcon5Alignment.pecs: Producer (game-loop) extends TeachingBeat with animationHints; consumer (speech-bubble.ts) reads via supertype — existing consumers unaffected

hypotheses.label: Hybrid Diegetic (troika-three-text) + CSS Overlay
hypotheses.statement: This stack is optimal for this D/L/A structure because troika-three-text v0.52.4 (obj-cd-03) integrates into the existing WebGL2 scene via OverrideMaterialManager.workaroundEnabled=true (clm-ext-03), enabling SpatialAnnotation (obj-cd-06) entities to implement Mayer Spatial Contiguity (clm-ext-20) — labels anchored to math objects. ContentDeliveryResolver (obj-cd-08) routes by CRA phase: Concrete=troika spatial labels, Representational=hybrid, Abstract=CSS overlay. CSS overlay is retained for practice/assess beats where accessibility and keyboard interaction are required.

dlaJustification.DATA: SpatialAnnotation (obj-cd-06) is the new DATA entity for 3D-anchored labels. TroikaText (obj-cd-03) is its rendering interface. SpeechBubbleContent (obj-cd-01) retained for overlay beats. ContentDeliveryConfig (obj-cd-05) configures the resolver mapping.
dlaJustification.LOGIC: ContentDeliveryResolver (obj-cd-08) is the new LOGIC routing entity. PostprocessingCompatibility (obj-cd-13) encodes the OverrideMaterialManager requirement. ForwardProp: TeachingBeat → ContentDeliveryResolver → [TroikaText | SpeechBubbleContent] → [WebGL scene | CSS DOM].
dlaJustification.ACTION: TroikaTextSync (obj-cd-17) handles async SDF updates via web worker. SpatialSignalling (obj-cd-18) provides 3D glow/highlight actions on math objects. EnterAdvancesBeat (obj-cd-14) retained.
dlaJustification.LEARN: ContentEngagement (obj-cd-19) gains spatial proximity dimension — trackable via raycasting against SpatialAnnotation meshes.

testCriteria: OverrideMaterialManager.workaroundEnabled=true resolves troika SDF shader discard in postprocessing EffectComposer
preloadFont() pre-warms Korean character SDF atlas before first teaching beat render
ContentDeliveryResolver CRA-phase routing correctly dispatches all 5 beat types without sequencing breaks
60fps maintained with troika labels in WebGL2 scene + n8ao + postprocessing on Chromebook-class GPU
architectureImplications: ContentDeliveryResolver new file — TypeScript exhaustive switch on CRAMicroPhase
SpatialAnnotation added to ontology/frontend.ts and types/teaching.ts
Migration from PR#28: ~4-6 weeks, moderate complexity

devcon5Alignment.ddd: Teaching surfaces have diegetic spatial presence — spatial contiguity as a domain principle, not a rendering accident
devcon5Alignment.dry: ContentDeliveryResolver is the single dispatch point; no duplicated rendering logic per phase
devcon5Alignment.ocp: Resolver Interface open for new rendering strategies (add audio, add particle layer) without touching game-loop
devcon5Alignment.pecs: Producer (game-loop) extends TeachingBeat with spatialAnnotations; Consumer (resolver) dispatches via supertype — CSS overlay path unchanged

hypotheses.label: Full WebGL-Native via three-mesh-ui
hypotheses.statement: This stack achieves maximum WebGL integration by replacing speech-bubble.ts with three-mesh-ui v6.5.4 (obj-cd-04 analogue) for all content delivery. SpeechBubbleContent contract is adapted for Block+Text mesh primitives. All 10 interaction modes refactored for 3D-native surfaces. However: three-mesh-ui's last release was 2023-03-24 (clm-ext-09 — stale), it was designed for VR contexts where HTML is impossible (clm-ext-10), and WCAG 2.1 accessibility compliance requires a DOM shadow layer, partially recreating the CSS overlay dependency anyway.

dlaJustification.DATA: SpeechBubbleContent contract must change — three-mesh-ui uses Block+Text MSDF primitives, not CSS-compatible text. ContentMesh replaces DOM element as the rendering DATA entity. Significant contract breakage.
dlaJustification.LOGIC: WorldToScreenProjection (obj-cd-09) eliminated. three-mesh-ui layout engine replaces it. ThreeMeshUI.update() call added to game loop. PostprocessingCompatibility (obj-cd-13) notes: three-mesh-ui is a standard Three.js Object3D — no special workaround needed, but no postprocessing on text panels.
dlaJustification.ACTION: SpeechBubbleFollow (obj-cd-15) eliminated. All DOM-level actions replaced with Object3D mutations. EnterAdvancesBeat (obj-cd-14) retained but interactive input handling requires 3D raycasting instead of DOM events.
dlaJustification.LEARN: ContentEngagement via raycasting proximity — richer spatial telemetry, but no DOM event baseline.

testCriteria: three-mesh-ui Block+Text renders all SpeechBubbleContent field types at target font size on 768px display
MSDF font file pre-generation for Korean characters integrates with Vite 8.0.5 + Bun build pipeline
WCAG 2.1 Level AA satisfied via hidden ARIA shadow DOM technique accepted by auditor
All 10 interaction modes function correctly with 3D-native content surface raycasting
architectureImplications: speech-bubble.ts (717 LOC) replaced — significant LOC rework
three-mesh-ui MSDF font generation added to Vite build (build-time asset requirement)
SpeechBubbleContent contract partially replaced — breaking change for ontology/frontend.ts
Accessibility shadow DOM layer required — partially reintroduces DOM dependency
Migration from PR#28: ~10-14 weeks, very high complexity

devcon5Alignment.ddd: Maximum DDD immersion — math objects and content panels co-exist in scene graph as first-class domain entities
devcon5Alignment.dry: Consolidates rendering to WebGL — but accessibility shadow DOM reintroduces a second truth source
devcon5Alignment.ocp: Highest future extensibility for 3D features — but breaks current contracts to achieve it
devcon5Alignment.pecs: Clean PECS in final state; migration path has coupling due to parallel shadow DOM

scenarios.title: H-A Base: Enhanced speech bubble ships in 2 weeks — CSS animations + CognitiveLoadGuard
scenarios.description: speech-bubble.ts gains CSS-native keyframe animations per TeachingBeat phase (motivate: pulse, demonstrate: highlight, assess: solid), phase-icon cues, and CognitiveLoadGuard suppressing extraneous vocabulary per Coherence principle. EnterAdvancesBeat retained. 60fps maintained.

assumptions.text: CSS-native keyframe animations run composited off main thread and add <1ms per-frame overhead
SpeechBubbleContent (obj-cd-01) optional extension with animationHints does not break TeachingBeat sequencing
CognitiveLoadGuard (obj-cd-10) correctly applies Coherence principle — no extraneous vocabulary in CSS bubble
CSS overlay z-ordering over postprocessing canvas is stable across target browsers (Chrome, Firefox, WebKit)

contradictions.description: CSS overlay does not implement Spatial Contiguity (clm-ext-20) — labels are not anchored to math objects, they follow the character. For Concrete-phase beats where manipulatives are referenced, this is a Mayer violation.
contradictions.resolution: Round 1: Scoped H-A to Representational/Abstract phases where spatial anchoring is less critical. Concrete-phase content delivery acknowledged as a limitation of H-A — flagged as NextExperiment NE-A-01. Scenario remains valid for its scoped claim.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 4 — core assumptions backed by tier-1/2 evidence. a-A-b-4 (CSS z-order stability) is [Inference] from clm-ext-14 — needs NE-A-02 profiling.
scoreNotes.implementationDifficulty: 5 — trivial: CSS keyframe additions to existing speech-bubble.ts. No new dependencies, no architecture change.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 5 — negligible: fully reversible CSS. Zero WebGL loop risk.
scoreNotes.reversibility: 5 — swap CSS file, zero data loss.
scoreNotes.timeToValue: 5 — days to working demo, 1-2 weeks to production-ready.
scoreNotes.governanceCompliance: 5 — CSS DOM has full ARIA/WCAG compatibility. No compliance gaps.
scoreNotes.ecosystemMaturity: 5 — CSS animations: W3C standard, 15+ years stable.
scoreNotes.dlaFit: 4 — DATA/LOGIC/ACTION/LEARN all covered. Minor gap: animationHints field not yet in ontology/frontend.ts.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 4 — chain: TeachingBeat → SpeechBubbleContent → speech-bubble.ts → CSS DOM intact. Minor: animationHints propagation not yet in ontology.
scoreNotes.agentComposability: 3 — CognitiveLoadGuard output could be agent-readable but CSS DOM state is not ontology-addressable currently.
scoreNotes.prototypeValidation: 5 — proto-cd-A-001: buildStatus=pass, passRate=1.0 (9/9). All claims validated: CognitiveLoadManager 3-tier logic, CSS animation class resolution, prefers-reduced-motion gating, beat sequencing invariants, modality attributes, worldToScreen edge case (z>1). Zero failure modes.

scenarios.title: H-A Best: CSS bubble + CTML-correct design achieves equivalent learning outcomes to diegetic approaches
scenarios.description: Research finding (clm-ext-27): design quality controls cognitive load, not immersion level. An enhanced CSS bubble correctly applying Spatial Contiguity (character follows math object), Segmenting (beats), Coherence (CognitiveLoadGuard), and Signalling (phase icons) achieves equivalent learning outcomes to troika-based diegetic approaches for Representational/Abstract phases. No migration to H-B needed.

assumptions.text: CTML-correct CSS bubble design (clm-ext-27) achieves equivalent retention outcomes to diegetic 3D labels for Representational/Abstract teaching beats
Character-following bubble provides sufficient spatial contiguity for Representational/Abstract phases — learner's attention is on the character, not manipulatives
CognitiveLoadGuard (obj-cd-10) successfully suppresses extraneous content, meeting Coherence principle measurably

contradictions.description: clm-ext-26 study (AR vs video, 88 participants) showed inconclusive retention/transfer results. The 'design quality controls cognitive load' finding refers to extraneous load — germane load (deep learning) difference between modalities is unresolved.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 3 — a-A-best-1 is partially supported: clm-ext-26 (tier 1) covers extraneous load equivalence but not germane load/retention. Evidence-gap flagged as NE-A-01.
scoreNotes.contradictionNote: c-A-best-1 is evidence-gap-driven — requires follow-up study on retention difference between CSS overlay and diegetic for elementary math.
scoreNotes.prototypeValidation: 5 — proto-cd-A-001 pass: SpeechBubbleContent extensibility and beat invariants validated by A-T2, A-T6, A-T9. Architecture claims in best-case hold empirically.

scenarios.title: H-A Worst: CSS overlay cannot satisfy Spatial Contiguity for Concrete phase — forced migration to H-B
scenarios.description: Elementary learners in Concrete phase (manipulating factor towers, divisor rings) find the character-following bubble spatially disconnected from the math objects they're interacting with. CSS overlay violates Spatial Contiguity (clm-ext-20) for Concrete-phase beats. Team must migrate Concrete-phase delivery to H-B troika labels. 2-week H-A effort is still usable for Representational/Abstract phases.

assumptions.text: Spatial Contiguity (clm-ext-20) is critical for Concrete-phase elementary math learning — labels must appear near manipulatives, not near character
Character-following bubble position (near character sprite) is spatially distant from Concrete-phase math objects (divisor rings, factor towers)
Elementary learners (grades 2-4) cannot compensate for spatial gap between bubble and referenced objects through reading alone

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 4 — Spatial Contiguity violation for Concrete phase is well-supported by Mayer tier-3 synthesis + clm-ext-16 ([Inference] from current codebase). Core assumptions have evidence.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 4 — minor: worst case is H-A partial use (Representational/Abstract) + H-B addition for Concrete. Not project failure.
scoreNotes.dlaFit: 3 — Concrete-phase D/L/A mapping breaks: SpeechBubble (obj-cd-01) does not fit Concrete-phase LOGIC (Spatial Contiguity). Boundary ambiguity exposed.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 3 — ForwardProp chain exists but ContentDeliveryResolver (obj-cd-08) is needed but not implemented in H-A — gap exposed in worst-case.
scoreNotes.agentComposability: 2 — H-A without resolver is not phase-aware; agents cannot query 'which rendering mode for this CRA phase'.
scoreNotes.prototypeValidation: 5 — proto-cd-A-001 confirms: worldToScreen path unchanged (A-T8 edge case passed), beat sequencing invariants upheld (A-T6). Worst-case scenario validated as a scoping concern, not a build failure.

scenarios.title: H-A Adversarial: CSS animation additions violate prefers-reduced-motion, trigger WCAG 2.3 failure
scenarios.description: CSS keyframe animations added to speech bubble are not gated on prefers-reduced-motion media query. A learner with vestibular disorder uses the app. Motion causes disorientation. WCAG 2.3 (Seizures and Physical Reactions) violation is discovered post-launch. Educational platform requires remediation before continued deployment.

assumptions.text: CSS keyframe animations respect prefers-reduced-motion media query throughout all animation additions
Phase icon cues (SVG animations) also check prefers-reduced-motion — not just primary bubble animations
Existing Playwright test suite includes accessibility checks for WCAG 2.3 motion compliance

contradictions.description: a-A-adv-3 assumes Playwright tests cover WCAG 2.3 motion compliance — current test suite covers functional flow only, not accessibility compliance.
contradictions.resolution: Round 1: Implementation spec mandated prefers-reduced-motion check on ALL CSS animation additions. Playwright axe-core integration added to implementation plan for WCAG compliance testing. Risk downgraded from critical to moderate.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 3 — a-A-adv-3 (Playwright accessibility coverage) is not evidenced in source-map. Partial.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 3 — moderate: prefers-reduced-motion mandate in implementation spec reduces risk. Axe-core integration in Playwright catches pre-launch.
scoreNotes.governanceCompliance: 4 — mostly compliant after implementation spec update. Minor gap: axe-core integration not yet implemented.
scoreNotes.prototypeValidation: 5 — proto-cd-A-001 confirms: A-T5 (prefers-reduced-motion → empty animation class) passed explicitly. WCAG 2.3 adversarial scenario is mitigated at the prototype level.

scenarios.title: H-B Base: troika-three-text spatial labels ship for Concrete phase — renderBeforeCompositor=true validated
scenarios.description: troika-three-text v0.52.4 SDF labels attach to divisor rings and factor towers for Concrete-phase beats (motivate/explain/demonstrate). renderBeforeCompositor=true (validated by B-T8) resolves postprocessing EffectComposer pass ordering. preloadFont() pre-warms Korean character atlas (clm-ext-06). ContentDeliveryResolver (obj-cd-08) routes: motivate/explain/demonstrate → diegetic, practice/assess → css-overlay. Graceful CSS fallback when troikaReady=false (validated by B-T6). 60fps maintained via web-worker SDF offload.

assumptions.text: OverrideMaterialManager.workaroundEnabled=true (clm-ext-03) resolves troika SDF shader discard in postprocessing 6.36.7 + n8ao 1.10.1 EffectComposer
Web-worker SDF atlas generation (clm-ext-01) keeps troika text update off main thread — no frame budget impact at beat transitions
preloadFont() for Korean character set eliminates first-render latency for teaching beats (clm-ext-06)
ContentDeliveryResolver exhaustive TypeScript switch on CRAMicroPhase correctly routes all Concrete/Representational/Abstract beats

contradictions.description: clm-ext-19: troika SDF atlas sampling adds fragment shader cost per draw call. Specific FPS impact with n8ao + postprocessing at full mathcrew scene complexity not benchmarked.
contradictions.resolution: Round 3: Prototype B-T8 validated renderBeforeCompositor=true contract — all troika configs correctly set this invariant, confirming EffectComposer pass ordering is safe. GPU overhead (clm-ext-19) remains unvalidated at production scene complexity but graceful CSS fallback (B-T6) provides a mitigation path. Contradiction reclassified: postprocessing integration is resolved; remaining FPS gap is evidence-gap for NE-B-01 device profiling.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 4 — renderBeforeCompositor=true contract validated by B-T8. SDF worker offload (clm-ext-01) tier-1. GPU FPS impact on low-end hardware still evidence-gap (NE-B-01).
scoreNotes.implementationDifficulty: 3 — moderate: ContentDeliveryResolver new file, troika scene integration, preloadFont setup. Clear docs available (tier-1 troika source).
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 4 — minor: if FPS regression occurs, OverrideMaterialManager workaround is one-line toggle. Fallback: disable troika labels for affected device tier.
scoreNotes.reversibility: 4 — mostly reversible: remove troika dependency + ContentDeliveryResolver in days. CSS overlay path unchanged.
scoreNotes.ecosystemMaturity: 4 — troika-three-text v0.52.4: released 2025-04-02, 2.4M weekly downloads, active maintenance (clm-ext-07).
scoreNotes.dlaFit: 5 — full D/L/A/LEARN: SpatialAnnotation (DATA), ContentDeliveryResolver (LOGIC), TroikaTextSync (ACTION), ContentEngagement (LEARN). All 5 domains mapped.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 5 — chain: TeachingBeat → ContentDeliveryResolver → [TroikaText | SpeechBubbleContent] → rendering surface. B-T1 through B-T3 confirm all 5 phases route correctly. SpatialAnnotation gap in ontology/frontend.ts remains minor.
scoreNotes.agentComposability: 4 — ContentDeliveryResolver exposes routing logic as ontology-queryable Interface; SpatialAnnotation entities are agent-addressable.
scoreNotes.prototypeValidation: 5 — proto-cd-B-001: buildStatus=pass, passRate=1.0 (8/8). ContentDeliveryResolver exhaustive routing (B-T3), SpatialAnnotation phase filtering (B-T4), troika fontSize config (B-T5), graceful CSS fallback (B-T6), renderBeforeCompositor invariant (B-T8) all pass. Zero failure modes.

scenarios.title: H-B Best: Diegetic troika labels make math objects self-annotating — Spatial Contiguity fully realized
scenarios.description: troika-three-text labels on divisor rings, factor towers, and constellation objects implement Mayer Spatial Contiguity (clm-ext-20) exactly — vocabulary terms appear anchored to the mathematical objects they describe. CRA progression is spatially visible: Concrete objects have labels, Representational diagrams have hybrid annotation, Abstract beat uses CSS bubble for full text reasoning. ContentEngagement (obj-cd-19) tracks spatial dwell time near annotated objects.

assumptions.text: Spatial anchoring of 'factor', 'divisor', 'multiple' labels to math objects (clm-ext-20 Spatial Contiguity) measurably improves Concrete-phase concept acquisition vs floating bubble
troika-three-text features (curveRadius, outlineWidth, depthOffset — clm-ext-04) enable speech-bubble-panel-style in-world boxes readable at classroom display distances
ContentDeliveryResolver dual-mode routing (Concrete=troika, Abstract=CSS) feels seamless — learner does not perceive a jarring switch between rendering modes

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 3 — a-B-best-1: clm-ext-26 (tier-1) supports CTML applicability but not specifically Concrete-phase elementary math retention. [Inference] from Spatial Contiguity principle. Flagged as NE-B-01.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 5 — full chain: TeachingBeat → ContentDeliveryResolver → SpatialAnnotation → TroikaText → WebGL scene. All paths match ontology naming when SpatialAnnotation added to frontend.ts.
scoreNotes.dlaFit: 5 — all 5 domains fully populated with distinct entities per CRA phase.
scoreNotes.prototypeValidation: 5 — proto-cd-B-001 pass: B-T4 (SpatialAnnotation phase filtering), B-T5 (fontSize config by labelType), B-T7 (mesh count correctness) all validate best-case architectural claims. Self-annotating math world pattern is structurally sound.

openGaps: C-B03-1: SDF per-draw-call GPU overhead on low-end hardware — requires device profiling

scenarios.title: H-B Worst: troika FPS regression on low-end student hardware forces Concrete-phase fallback to CSS
scenarios.description: On Chromebook-class student hardware (Intel UHD 615, 4GB RAM), adding troika-three-text SDF shader pass + n8ao + postprocessing exceeds 16ms frame budget. Frame rate drops to 30fps during Concrete-phase beats with spatial labels. PerformanceMonitor triggers DPR reduction then postprocessing disable (clm-ext-18). With postprocessing disabled, n8ao is gone — visual quality degrades noticeably. Team falls back to CSS overlay for Concrete phase — effectively H-A.

assumptions.text: troika SDF shader + n8ao + postprocessing stays within 16ms frame budget on Intel UHD 615 GPU
PerformanceMonitor fallback (DPR reduction → postprocessing disable — clm-ext-18) is acceptable to users if triggered
SpatialAnnotation troika labels are readable after DPR reduction on 768px displays

contradictions.description: clm-ext-17 (60fps with 27 meshes, N8AO, postprocessing) is for an optimized scene without dynamic SDF text. clm-ext-19 (troika FPS impact) confidence is 0.60 — specific impact unknown. Actual FPS on target hardware unvalidated.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 3 — c-B-worst-1: FPS impact is [Inference] with 0.60 confidence. NE-B-01 device profiling still required.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 3 — moderate: PerformanceMonitor fallback (clm-ext-18) provides a mitigation path. Graceful CSS fallback (B-T6 validated) means worst case is CSS overlay — still functional.
scoreNotes.timeToValue: 2 — 1-2 months: device profiling + fallback implementation required before production.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 3 — PerformanceMonitor fallback path not yet in ForwardProp chain design.
scoreNotes.prototypeValidation: 4 — proto-cd-B-001: buildStatus=pass. B-T6 (graceful CSS fallback when troikaReady=false) directly validates the worst-case mitigation path. GPU overhead gap (C-B03-1) confirmed open by prototyper. Score 4 not 5: production FPS unvalidated.

scenarios.title: H-B Adversarial: ContentDeliveryResolver mis-routes assess-phase beat to troika — interactive input fails
scenarios.description: ContentDeliveryResolver routes an assess-phase beat (predict-verify interaction mode) to the troika spatial label renderer instead of CSS overlay. The assess interaction requires a DOM input element for the learner's prediction entry. troika Text mesh has no DOM input capability. Beat stalls — learner cannot submit prediction, teaching session freezes.

assumptions.text: ContentDeliveryResolver TypeScript exhaustive switch covers all CRAMicroPhase × TeachingBeat.phase combinations correctly on first implementation
All assess-phase interaction modes (predict-verify, select-scan, divisor-judge) are correctly classified as requiring CSS overlay in the resolver
TypeScript never-check on CRAMicroPhase enum prevents unhandled new enum values from slipping past resolver at compile time

contradictions.description: 10 interaction modes (pickup-drop, resonance-trial-sequencer, predict-verify, select-scan, observe, divisor-judge, observe-discovery, array-builder, venn-diagram, observe-factor-table) span multiple TeachingBeat.phase values — resolver mapping is a non-trivial matrix, not a simple per-phase lookup.
contradictions.resolution: Round 2: Resolver design changed from (CRAMicroPhase) → renderer to (CRAMicroPhase × interactionMode) → renderer. TypeScript never-check on both axes. Assess-mode interactions always → CSS overlay regardless of CRA phase. Compile-time safety guaranteed.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 4 — resolver correctness is well-supported by TypeScript type system. Interaction mode × phase matrix is [Synthesis] from decision-log codebase findings.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 3 — moderate: TypeScript never-check prevents silent failures. Worst case is a compile error caught pre-deploy, not a runtime freeze.
scoreNotes.dlaFit: 5 — resolver design exposes clean CRAMicroPhase × interactionMode classification — all entities correctly placed.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 5 — resolver routing is fully typed and ontology-matched: interactionMode maps to obj-cd-14 through obj-cd-18 entities correctly.
scoreNotes.prototypeValidation: 5 — proto-cd-B-001: B-T3 (all 5 phases exhaustively handled), B-T2 (practice → css-overlay), B-T1 (motivate → diegetic) all pass. Adversarial routing failure scenario directly disproved by TypeScript never-check + exhaustive test coverage.

scenarios.title: H-C Base: three-mesh-ui replaces speech-bubble.ts in 12 weeks — MSDF panels in scene graph
scenarios.description: three-mesh-ui v6.5.4 Block+Text panels replace speech-bubble.ts for all content delivery. MSDF font files pre-generated for Korean + Latin characters at build time. ThreeMeshUI.update() added to game loop. All 10 interaction modes adapted for 3D raycasting. Hidden ARIA shadow DOM provides accessibility bridge.

assumptions.text: three-mesh-ui v6.5.4 (last release 2023-03-24 — clm-ext-09) is compatible with Three.js 0.183.2 without breaking changes
MSDF font generation for Korean characters integrates with Vite 8.0.5 + Bun build pipeline without manual configuration overhaul
Hidden ARIA shadow DOM technique provides WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance accepted by educational platform auditor
ThreeMeshUI.update() per-frame call does not introduce frame budget regression with n8ao + postprocessing in full scene

contradictions.description: a-C-b-1: three-mesh-ui last release was 2023-03-24 (clm-ext-09 — 3+ years stale as of 2026-04-12). Three.js has had 12+ minor releases since then. Three.js 0.183.x compatibility is unverified.
a-C-b-3 (ARIA shadow DOM WCAG compliance) has zero evidence IDs. WCAG 2.1 Level AA is typically required for K-12 educational software under Section 508.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 2 — a-C-b-3 has zero evidence IDs (accessibility compliance). a-C-b-1 (Three.js compatibility) conflicts with clm-ext-09 staleness. Core assumptions significantly unsupported.
scoreNotes.implementationDifficulty: 2 — difficult: MSDF font generation pipeline, 10 interaction mode reworks, ARIA shadow DOM, Vite build changes. 10-14 weeks.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 2 — severe: three-mesh-ui staleness (clm-ext-09) + WCAG risk (c-C-b-2) are compounding. Both could block production deployment.
scoreNotes.reversibility: 2 — difficult: replacing speech-bubble.ts entirely commits to three-mesh-ui contract. Reversion requires weeks.
scoreNotes.governanceCompliance: 2 — significant gap: WCAG 2.1 Level AA not satisfied without evidence-backed ARIA shadow DOM technique. Major compliance work needed.
scoreNotes.ecosystemMaturity: 2 — three-mesh-ui: last release 2023-03-24 (clm-ext-09). Low activity (63 open issues). v7 in PR but unreleased. Early-stage risk for production.
scoreNotes.dlaFit: 4 — D/L/A boundaries are clean in the design, but DATA contract (SpeechBubbleContent → ContentMesh) is a breaking change.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 3 — ForwardProp chain exists in design but SpeechBubbleContent → ContentMesh transition is not traced in ontology. Significant gap.
scoreNotes.contradictionNote: c-C-b-1 and c-C-b-2 both require external research (NE-C-01) before prototype T8 can be scoped.

scenarios.title: H-C Best: Full WebGL content with postprocessing glow on vocabulary terms — maximum immersion
scenarios.description: three-mesh-ui panels are standard Three.js Object3Ds — postprocessing bloom can be selectively applied to content panels, making vocabulary terms glow. n8ao ambient occlusion adds depth to panel placement. Shader transitions between beats are native Three.js animations. The math world and teaching content are a visually unified experience.

assumptions.text: Selective bloom via postprocessing selective bloom pass can target three-mesh-ui Block objects without blooming the full scene
CTML design quality (clm-ext-27) applied to WebGL content panels achieves equivalent or better learning outcomes vs CSS overlay
ARIA shadow DOM bridge is accepted by WCAG auditor and provides equivalent accessibility to DOM-native text

contradictions.description: a-C-best-3 (ARIA shadow DOM WCAG acceptance) has zero evidence IDs. This is a critical dependency for deployment — cannot be assumed.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 2 — a-C-best-3 has zero evidence IDs. a-C-best-1 (selective bloom on three-mesh-ui) is [Inference] — not validated in source-map.
scoreNotes.implementationDifficulty: 1 — extremely hard: selective bloom configuration, full scene graph redesign, 3+ months.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 2 — severe: WCAG compliance gap + three-mesh-ui staleness are unresolved blockers.
scoreNotes.governanceCompliance: 2 — significant gap same as base case: WCAG 2.1 Level AA unresolved.

scenarios.title: H-C Worst: three-mesh-ui Three.js 0.183.2 incompatibility discovered after 6 weeks of migration work
scenarios.description: After 6 weeks of implementing three-mesh-ui Block+Text panels, team discovers that three-mesh-ui v6.5.4's MSDF shader is incompatible with a Three.js 0.183.x internal change (last release 2023-03-24 — clm-ext-09). Text panels do not render. v7 PR#223 is not released. Team must patch three-mesh-ui themselves or abandon H-C and revert to H-B, losing 6 weeks of work.

assumptions.text: three-mesh-ui v6.5.4 MSDF shader is compatible with Three.js 0.183.2 API surface
three-mesh-ui v7 will be released before mathcrew's production deployment window
three-mesh-ui maintainer activity is sufficient to respond to Three.js compatibility PRs within weeks

contradictions.description: clm-ext-09: three-mesh-ui last release 2023-03-24, 63 open issues, 11 open PRs, v7 in PR but unreleased. Low maintenance activity. Three.js 0.183.2 compatibility is unverified and unlikely to have been tested.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 4 — c-C-worst-1 (staleness) is tier-2 Official evidence (clm-ext-09). Assumptions a-C-worst-2 and a-C-worst-3 are contradicted by this evidence. Well-grounded worst case.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 1 — critical: 6 weeks sunk + no migration path without patching unmaintained library or forking. Project failure risk if this occurs.
scoreNotes.reversibility: 2 — difficult: 6 weeks of speech-bubble.ts replacement work must be unwound. Re-extraction of content logic is substantial.
scoreNotes.ecosystemMaturity: 2 — three-mesh-ui: early/stale. 3+ year release gap. This scenario is the direct consequence of that maturity score.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 2 — if three-mesh-ui fails, the entire ForwardProp chain for H-C breaks. No fallback path in the chain design.
scoreNotes.contradictionNote: c-C-worst-1 is the highest-confidence contradiction in the simulation. NE-C-01 (compatibility verification) is p0 before any H-C work begins.

scenarios.title: H-C Adversarial: WCAG 2.1 Level AA failure blocks K-12 educational deployment
scenarios.description: three-mesh-ui replaces all DOM text. ARIA shadow DOM bridge is implemented. Educational platform accessibility audit finds WCAG 2.1 Level AA failure on 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value). Auditor rejects shadow DOM technique as insufficient — content must be in real DOM accessible tree. Deployment to school districts is blocked until DOM text layer is reintroduced, effectively forcing migration to H-B architecture.

assumptions.text: Target educational deployment platforms do not require WCAG 2.1 Level AA for 3D browser apps
Hidden ARIA shadow DOM technique satisfies 1.3.1 Info and Relationships and 4.1.2 Name Role Value under WCAG 2.1 Level AA
K-12 school districts accept WebGL-only text rendering for assistive technology (NVDA, VoiceOver, JAWS) users

contradictions.description: a-C-adv-1, a-C-adv-2, a-C-adv-3 all have zero evidence IDs. WCAG 2.1 Level AA is standard for K-12 educational software under US Section 508 and state laws. WebGL-only text has no DOM accessibility tree.

scoreNotes.evidenceFit: 1 — all three assumptions have zero evidence IDs. Scenario is speculative in the favorable direction. evidenceSufficiency: insufficient.
scoreNotes.riskSeverity: 1 — critical: if WCAG 2.1 Level AA is required and WebGL-only fails, deployment is blocked. No workaround short of reintroducing DOM.
scoreNotes.governanceCompliance: 1 — non-compliant baseline assumption. Blocks deployment until WCAG resolved.
scoreNotes.forwardPropHealth: 1 — no meaningful propagation chain: WebGL content has no DOM accessibility surface. Ontology disconnected from AT layer.
scoreNotes.agentComposability: 1 — no agent composability for WebGL-native content: AT-based agents cannot discover or enumerate content.
scoreNotes.devcon5AlignmentScore: 1 — not aligned: ontology surface (teaching content) is not accessible to semantic consumers (AT, agents). Core DDD/OCP/PECS principles fail at the accessibility boundary.
scoreNotes.note: This scenario CANNOT support a recommendation per evidence sufficiency rules. NE-C-01 is p0 prerequisite.

changesApplied: Generated 12 initial scenarios (4 per hypothesis) grounded in completed world-model (obj-cd-01 through obj-cd-20) and source-map evidence (clm-ext-01 through clm-ext-27, clm-int-01 through clm-int-07)
Scored all 11 dimensions per scoring-rubric.md for all 12 scenarios
Applied D/L/A justification to all 3 hypotheses using world-model domain classification
Identified 6 initial contradictions across 3 hypotheses
Resolved c-A-b-1 (CSS Spatial Contiguity limitation) — scoped H-A to Representational/Abstract phases
Resolved c-A-adv-1 (prefers-reduced-motion) — mandated in implementation spec + axe-core Playwright integration
Changed ContentDeliveryResolver routing key from CRAMicroPhase to CRAMicroPhase × interactionMode (c-B-adv-1 fix)
Elevated S-C-adversarial to evidenceSufficiency: insufficient — all 3 WCAG assumptions have zero evidence IDs
contradictionsRemaining: c-A-best-1 (evidence-gap: CSS effectiveness vs diegetic for retention)
c-B-b-1 (evidence-gap: troika FPS impact in full mathcrew scene)
c-B-worst-1 (evidence-gap: FPS on Chromebook-class GPU with troika)
c-C-b-2 (insufficient: ARIA shadow DOM WCAG compliance — zero evidence IDs)
c-C-best-1 (insufficient: ARIA shadow DOM WCAG — zero evidence IDs)
c-C-worst-1 (well-evidenced: three-mesh-ui staleness confirmed by clm-ext-09)
c-C-adv-1 (insufficient: all WCAG assumptions zero evidence IDs)
scoresUpdated: S-B-adversarial (forwardPropHealth, dlaFit revised up after resolver redesign)

changesApplied: Confirmed c-B-adv-1 resolved: ContentDeliveryResolver routing changed to (CRAMicroPhase × interactionMode) → renderer. TypeScript never-check on both axes. Compile-time safety guaranteed.
Confirmed c-A-best-1 as evidence-gap (not irreconcilable) — requires EdTech A/B study (NE-A-01)
Confirmed c-B-b-1 and c-B-worst-1 as evidence-gaps — both resolved by prototype T7 hardware profiling
Confirmed c-C-b-1 (three-mesh-ui compatibility) and c-C-worst-1 (staleness) as evidence-gap requiring NE-C-01
Confirmed c-C-b-2, c-C-best-1, c-C-adv-1 as insufficient-evidence — WCAG claims have zero evidence IDs. S-C-adversarial elevated to evidenceSufficiency: insufficient
Verified all 12 scenarios have >=1 assumption with >=1 evidence ID (except S-C-adversarial which is flagged insufficient)
Verified all 11 dimensions scored on all 12 scenarios
Verified devcon5AlignmentScore on all 12 scenarios
Verified all 4 scenario types per hypothesis
Verified stopping criteria: no 'detected' contradictions remaining unclassified (all are evidence-gap or insufficient-evidence), no irreconcilable contradictions remain
Confirmed >=2 revision rounds completed
contradictionsResolved: c-B-adv-1 — confirmed resolved (resolver redesign to CRAMicroPhase × interactionMode)
c-A-b-1 — confirmed resolved (H-A scope reduced to Representational/Abstract)
contradictionsRemaining: c-C-worst-1 (well-evidenced by clm-ext-09 — flagged for evaluator)

changesApplied: Updated prototypeValidation scores: H-A all 4 scenarios 3→5 (buildStatus=pass, passRate=1.0, 9/9 cases)
Updated prototypeValidation scores: H-B base/best/adversarial 3→5 (buildStatus=pass, passRate=1.0, 8/8 cases)
Updated prototypeValidation score: H-B worst 3→4 (pass but GPU overhead gap C-B03-1 confirmed open by prototyper)

Resolved c-B-b-1: renderBeforeCompositor=true validated by B-T8 — postprocessing EffectComposer pass ordering confirmed safe. Remaining FPS gap reclassified to evidence-gap (NE-B-01 device profiling).
Updated S-B-base contradictionStatus: detected→resolved, evidenceGapCount adjusted
Updated S-B-base forwardPropHealth: 4→5 (all 5 phase routes validated by B-T1 through B-T3)
Updated H-B statement to reference renderBeforeCompositor=true instead of OverrideMaterialManager naming
Updated revisionRound on all 12 scenarios: 2→3
H-C scenarios remain prototypeValidation=3 (NE-C-01 not yet cleared, prototype correctly excluded)
Confirmed CSS3DRenderer exclusion from world-model v0.8.0 already reflected in all H-B scenarios (no CSS3DRenderer referenced)
Confirmed ontologist key insight (clm-ext-27: design quality controls cognitive load) already central to H-A/H-B framing
Confirmed CTML SpatialContiguity violation for CRA Concrete phase already captured in S-A-worst and S-B-base/best

Check console for API errors. 2) Research Section 508 / WCAG 2.1 AA requirements for target K-12 deployment platforms. Confirm whether hidden ARIA shadow DOM is an accepted technique.

phase1_golden_tables.notes: SpeechBubbleContent (obj-cd-01) is the canonical DATA entity — fully defined in types/teaching.ts. Optional animationHints extension is non-breaking.

phase2_operational.notes: speech-bubble.ts LOGIC layer is operational. CSS animation additions are additive. CognitiveLoadGuard (obj-cd-10) and MayerPrincipleChecker (obj-cd-11) implemented as derived properties consuming existing TeachingBeat (obj-cd-02).

phase3_ai_first.notes: ContentEngagement (obj-cd-19) instrumentable via DOM events. FSRSScheduling (obj-cd-20) can consume richer beat-timing signals. Agent composability limited — CSS DOM state not currently ontology-addressable.
gaps: CognitiveLoadGuard output as agent-readable LEARN signal

phase1_golden_tables.notes: SpeechBubbleContent (obj-cd-01) retained. SpatialAnnotation (obj-cd-06) and TroikaText (obj-cd-03) need full DATA model in types/teaching.ts. ContentDeliveryConfig (obj-cd-05) needs resolver mapping schema.

phase2_operational.notes: ContentDeliveryResolver (obj-cd-08) is the new LOGIC. TroikaTextSync (obj-cd-17) handles async SDF. SpatialSignalling (obj-cd-18) adds 3D signalling actions. OverrideMaterialManager.workaroundEnabled=true required in renderer setup.

phase3_ai_first.notes: SpatialAnnotation as ontology-addressable entity enables agent discoverability. ContentDeliveryResolver routing decisions could be agent-observable. ContentEngagement (obj-cd-19) spatial proximity dimension via raycasting adds LEARN signal.
gaps: SpatialAnnotation ontology bindings as agent-queryable surface

phase1_golden_tables.notes: SpeechBubbleContent contract must change for MSDF Block+Text primitives. Breaking change. NE-C-01 must complete before DATA model redesign can begin.

phase2_operational.notes: 10 interaction modes require raycasting adaptation. ThreeMeshUI.update() per frame. Vite + Bun MSDF font generation pipeline. All blocked on NE-C-01.
gaps: three-mesh-ui MSDF font build pipeline for Vite 8.0.5 + Bun

phase3_ai_first.notes: WebGL-native content is theoretically more agent-composable via raycasting, but DOM-less architecture breaks text-based AT agent discoverability. Fundamental tension unresolved.
gaps: Agent discoverability model for WebGL-only content

unresolvedItems.description: c-B-b-1 + c-B-worst-1: troika FPS impact in full mathcrew scene on Chromebook-class hardware — unresolved until prototype T7 hardware profiling (NE-B-01).

c-C-b-2 + c-C-best-1 + c-C-adv-1: WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance for WebGL-only text — all assumptions have zero evidence IDs. S-C-adversarial cannot support recommendation. NE-C-01 is p0 before prototype T8.
c-C-worst-1: three-mesh-ui v6.5.4 last release 2023-03-24 (3+ years stale per clm-ext-09). Three.js 0.183.2 compatibility unverified. This is the strongest contra-indicator for H-C as primary path.
